name: Prune and Almond Tart
recipeIngredient: 5 dl Brewed hot strong black tea (such as Earl Grey)
1 Flaky sweet pastry shell, partially baked and cooled
recipeInstructions:
1. Convert measurements: 5 dl hot tea = 2 cups; 35 g almonds = 1/4 cup; 1 7/8 dl cream = 3/4 cup.
2. Make a strong black tea by using about 1 tablespoon of loose Earl Grey to 5 dl of water.
3. Pour the hot tea over the prunes and set aside to marinate for at least 1 hour before preparing the tart.
4. Preheat the oven to 190°C (375°F).
5. Grind the almonds to a fine powder.
6. Add the egg, sugar, eau-de-vie or brandy, and cream. Process until very smooth.
7. Thoroughly drain the prunes, discarding the soaking liquid. Carefully arrange the prunes in the cooled tart shell (2 layers).
8. Pour the almond filling over the prunes.
9. Place the tart in the center of the oven and bake until the filling is set and the tart shell is nicely browned, about 45 minutes.
10. Remove from the oven and sprinkle with confectioners' sugar. Cool on a rack.
11. Serve at room temperature.
